LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS REGARDING HOW DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS CAUSED HIM DAMAGE. THERE IS NO APPARENT CAUSAL NEXUS BETWEEN DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS AND THE PRE-EXISTING GLENDONBROOK JUDGMENT. THE JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED AGAINST PLAINTIFF PRIOR TO THE ALLEGEDLY FRAUDULENT SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS IN THE MALPRACTICE ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS CAUSED PLAINTIFF TO LOSE THE MALPRACTICE SETTLEMENT PROCEEDS. PLAINTIFF, HOWEVER, RESCINDED THE SETTLEMENT AND ALSO CONCEDES THAT THE COURT ORDERED THE SETTLEMENT ENFORCEABLE. AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY AUGUST 11, 2014. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT ESTABLISHES THAT THIS IS A SLAPP SUIT. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BASED ON DEFENDANT'S LEGAL REPRESENTATION OF GLENDONBROOK WINES. "ALL COMMUNICATIVE ACTS PERFORMED BY ATTORNEYS AS PART OF THEIR REPRESENTATION OF A CLIENT IN A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING OR OTHER PETITIONING CONTEXT ARE PER SE PROTECTED AS PETITIONING ACTIVITY BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE." CABRAL V. MARTINS (2009) 177 CAL.APP.4TH 471, 480. THEREFORE, THE BURDEN HAS SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SHOWING A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MAINTAIN HIS BURDEN. FIRST, HE DOES NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE, BUT MERELY RELIES ON HIS UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT. SECOND, CIVIL COD 47 [LITIGATION PRIVILEGE] BARS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is taken off calendar per request of the moving party. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JOHN GIDDING, TEL. NO. (212) 495-9422, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. LINDSEY A. MORGAN OF THE SINNOTT, PUEBLA, CAMPAGNE & CURET LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 352-6202 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. Defendant establishes that this is a SLAPP suit. Plaintiff's claims are based on defendant's retention of counsel (Lewis Brisbois) and defendant's litigation conduct in the malpractice action (case #507519-Gidding v. Salama). "All communicative acts performed by attorneys as part of their representation of a client in a judicial proceeding or other petitioning context are per se protected as petitioning activity by the anti-SLAPP statute." Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 480. Therefore, the burden has shifted to plaintiff to present evidence showing a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has failed to maintain his burden. First, plaintiff fails to present evidence to support the claims pleaded in the amended complaint. Plaintiff's opposition focuses on tax fraud claims, but the amended complaint does not allege such claims. Second, Civil Code 47 [litigation privilege] bars plaintiff's claims. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kendra L. Basner, Esq. of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP, Tel. 415.362.6000, for defendant and moving party;
John Gidding, Tel. 212.495.9422, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf by Cour Call.

RULING: The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Off calendar as moot. One order signed for both motions. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kendra L. Basner, Esq. of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP, Tel. 415.362.6000, for defendant and moving party;
John Gidding, Tel. 212.495.9422, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf by Cour Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted. Defendant establishes that this is a SLAPP suit. Plaintiff's claims are based on defendant's legal representation of Glendonbrook Wines. "All communicative acts performed by attorneys as part of their representation of a client in a judicial proceeding or other petitioning context are per se protected as petitioning activity by the anti-SLAPP statute." Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 480. Therefore, the burden has shifted to plaintiff to present evidence showing a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has failed to maintain his burden. First, he does not present any evidence, but merely relies on his unverified complaint. Second, Civil Cod 47 [litigation privilege] bars plaintiff's claims. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JOHN GIDDING, TEL. NO. (212) 495-9422, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. LINDSEY A. MORGAN OF SINNOTT, PUEBLA, CAMPAGNE & CURET, TEL. NO. 952-6202 APPEARING FOR THE DEFEDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead specific allegations regarding how defendant's alleged misrepresentations caused him damage. There is no apparent causal nexus between defendant's alleged misrepresentations and the pre-existing Glendonbrook judgment. The judgment was entered against plaintiff prior to the allegedly fraudulent settlement discussions in the malpractice action. Plaintiff alleges that the alleged misrepresentations caused plaintiff to lose the malpractice settlement proceeds. Plaintiff, however, rescinded the settlement and also concedes that the court ordered the settlement enforceable. Amended complaint to be filed by August 11, 2014. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/pjb
